This is the textbook intended workflow for a binary-catalyst miss. (b) **Mechanical risk-management converted a -16% mark-to-mark gap-down into a flat realized outcome on the remainder** â€” the 5-share AVGO position's structural alpha came from the Tue partial-profit gate (+$141.68 locked) which fired BEFORE the print; the trail trip on the remainder produced essentially break-even (-$1.26) instead of the full -16% gap-down hit that an unprotected position would have absorbed. **The strategy worked exactly as designed: harvest partial gain pre-binary, let trail stop manage post-binary downside.** (c) **First closed long-only swing-trade position in the agent's history with a positive realized return** ($140.42 / +6.83% over 16 trading days = ~82% annualized on positioned capital, EXCLUDING cash sleeve) â€” Bull is no longer "$0 realized through 4 weeks"; the discipline + mechanical risk-management thesis is now data-backed. (d) **Background-poll architecture worked for verifying the open print** â€” set a poll at market-open + 2 min, monitor every 30s, exit on order-status change; the poll caught the transition from `new` to `FILLED_OR_GONE` between 09:33 and 09:33:47 ET (a 30-second resolution window) and the post-fill state verification followed cleanly. (e) **No discretionary override of the mechanical exit even though the fill price ($410.57) is well below the notional trail trigger (~$443.13)** â€” the gap-down ate through the trigger before the market opened; the strategy's response was a market sell at the open print, NOT a "wait for a bounce to fill higher" override. Discipline held. **What didn't work**: (a) **Trail-stop trigger gap is the structural limitation** â€” the notional trigger was ~$443.13 (10% below Wed's ~$492.81 HWM), but the gap-down opened AVGO at ~$410.57 = the market sell filled $32.56/sh / -7.4% below the notional trigger. **For binary-catalyst-day positions, the trail stop does NOT guarantee a fill at the trigger price; it guarantees a market sell when the trigger is breached.** This is a textbook gap-risk limitation of trail stops, but it cost $97.68 of "expected" protected downside on the 3-share tranche. (b) **No standing limit-sell order at the notional trigger** â€” would not have helped here (limit at ~$443 would have failed to fill on a gap-down to $410), but for asymmetric gap-down scenarios, a stop-LIMIT might have provided more control. Carry to weekly review: stop-limit vs trail-stop on binary-catalyst-day positions. (c) **Same-day SPY anchor not pulled this session** â€” will need to source at midday or close for the day-alpha computation. (d) **TZ display bug persists** (snapshot showed "13:53 ET" for actual ~09:53 ET = +4-hour UTC offset still applied). (e) **Operational backlog 35 days old unchanged** â€” Alpaca SPY snapshot pull, operator-decision items ($10k baseline + UTC-timestamp), `alpaca_client.py` cancel JSONDecodeError + `--qty` flag. **One thing to try differently next time**: **For positions held into a binary print, evaluate at pre-market whether to convert the trail stop to a stop-LIMIT order with a wider band** (e.g., stop at notional trigger, limit at 5-7% below trigger to absorb gap-down volatility while preventing fills below a hard floor). On today's case: a stop-LIMIT with trigger $443 / limit $410 would have allowed the $410.57 fill we got; a stop-LIMIT with trigger $443 / limit $430 would have NOT filled (preventing the loss but leaving the position exposed) â€” neither is unambiguously better. Carry to Fri 6/5 weekly review as a formal item: **"Trail-stop vs stop-LIMIT for binary-catalyst-day positions: what's the right primary mechanism?"** **Next on-cron fire**: Thu 6/4 ~12:00 ET midday â€” priority queue: (1) reconcile SPY day return for clean day-alpha computation; (2) verify 0 positions / 100% cash state; (3) confirm no new entries today (post-disappointment AI-semi correlation veto); (4) Fri 6/5 weekly review prep â€” the formal recalibration question is now answered (partial-profit + trail-stop sequence locked +$140.42 on a 16-day catalyst window = patience-mode discipline vindicated with data). Then Thu 6/4 ~15:00 ET market-close = full day-alpha computation + ClickUp EOD send + Week 4 cumulative-alpha tally + Fri 6/5 weekly review prep.

This is the structural insurance trade-off being made by the cash sleeve â€” quantifying it on both up-days and down-days produces a fairer cumulative picture than just tracking realized alpha.
- **Lesson / Heuristic**: **Alpha is asymmetric for cash-heavy portfolios â€” track the dollar magnitude of forgone-vs-captured alpha on each side, not just signed alpha.** Today: forgone +$850 on a +0.89% SPY up-day (we'd have captured this at full exposure). Mon: captured +$160 alpha on a âˆ’0.16% SPY day. Tue: captured +$520 alpha on a âˆ’0.52% SPY day. Week 2 net dollar alpha: +$160 + $520 âˆ’ $850 = **âˆ’$170 dollar-weighted** through Wed close, even though signed-percent alpha is still net positive (a function of small base values Ã— small percentages). The percent-alpha framing can flatter cash sleeves; dollar-alpha is the honest measure. **Heuristic added**: at every close session, compute both signed-percent-alpha AND dollar-alpha (signed_pct Ã— prior_close_equity) for the day, and the running dollar-alpha for the week â€” this is the cleanest measure of whether discipline cost or saved real money.
